Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First the activities involved are RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP Let me list them out again to make sure I have them all

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me think about the typical flow of an order fulfillment process The process probably starts with RO Then CA must happen after RO Then RI after CA Then PI after RI Then QC after PI Then PO after QC Then GSL after PO Then D after GSL Then IC and RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before If the first activity occurs then the second must have been executed before For example if CA occurs then RO must have been executed before So the tuple would be CA RO in always before

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Similarly for other steps Let us think CA RO RI CA PI RI QC PI PO QC GSL PO D GSL IC D RP D RP IC

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Typically after the order is dispatched you invoice the customer and record payment The scenario is not explicit Let us assume that IC and RP happen after D

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After As discussed each activity must be followed by the next So the pairs are RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The always after constraint does not require immediate succession just that the second occurs after the first But in the process described the sequence is linear RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Never Together Are there any such pairs here Perhaps there are no never together constraints here

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Since all activities are part of the process there are no pairs that cannot co exist So never together is empty

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Each case is an order So RO must occur exactly once Then CA must occur once per order Similarly RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The scenario says must be processed through a series of activities implying all steps are required So each activity must occur exactly once So activ freq for all activities is 1

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Equivalence Since all activities are required once all pairs would be equivalent But that would be redundant So perhaps the equivalence set is empty

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Directly Follows All consecutive steps in the process RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Putting all this together the final Log Skeleton would have equivalence empty always before with the listed precedence pairs always after with the listed succession pairs never together empty activ freq all activities mapped to 1 and directly follows with the consecutive pairs
